description: Provides tools for user authentication, contact management, and message operations in a messaging workspace. Supports login/logout, sending and receiving messages, viewing inboxes, searching message history, and retrieving statistics. Useful for building chat applications and messaging integrations.

tools.name: login
tools.description: Use this tool to authenticate and log in a user to the messaging system. Requires a user ID and returns authentication status along with the logged-in user ID if successful.

user_id.description: The unique identifier of the user attempting to log in to the messaging system. This ID is used to authenticate the user session.

success.description: Indicates whether the login attempt was successful (true) or failed (false).

user_id.description: The unique identifier of the user who successfully logged in. This field is only present when success is true.

message.description: A human-readable status message describing the outcome of the login attempt, such as success or error details.

tools.name: logout
tools.description: Use this tool to log out the currently authenticated user from the messaging system. Returns logout success status.

success.description: Indicates whether the logout operation was successful (true) or failed (false).

message.description: A human-readable status message describing the outcome of the logout operation.

tools.name: check_login_status
tools.description: Use this tool to check the current login status of the user in the messaging system. Returns whether a user is logged in and the current user ID if authenticated.

is_logged_in.description: Indicates whether a user is currently authenticated and logged in to the messaging system.

user_id.description: The unique identifier of the currently logged-in user. This field is only present when is_logged_in is true.

tools.name: list_users
tools.description: Use this tool to retrieve a list of all users available in the messaging workspace. Returns an array of user objects containing usernames and user IDs that can be used for login and messaging operations.

username.description: The human-readable username of the user in the messaging workspace.

user_id.description: The unique identifier assigned to the user, used for login and messaging operations.

tools.name: get_user_id
tools.description: Use this tool to retrieve the user ID for a given username. Returns the user ID and username, which can be used for login and sending messages.

username.description: The username to look up in the messaging workspace. This is used to retrieve the corresponding unique user ID.

user_id.description: The unique identifier corresponding to the requested username, used for login and sending messages.

username.description: The username that was looked up, confirming the mapping.

tools.name: add_contact
tools.description: Use this tool to add a new contact to the messaging workspace. The system automatically assigns a unique user ID. Returns the new contact's details and creation status.

username.description: The username for the new contact to be added to the messaging workspace. The system will automatically assign a unique user ID to this contact.

success.description: Indicates whether the contact was successfully added to the workspace (true) or failed (false).

username.description: The username of the newly added contact.

user_id.description: The unique identifier automatically assigned to the new contact, used for login and messaging operations.

message.description: A human-readable status message describing the outcome of the contact addition.

tools.name: send_message
tools.description: Use this tool to send a message to another user in the messaging system. Requires authentication via login. Returns message delivery status and metadata including a unique message ID for future reference.

receiver_id.description: The unique user ID of the message recipient. This ID identifies which user will receive the message.

message.description: The content of the message to be sent to the recipient. This text forms the body of the communication.

success.description: Indicates whether the message was successfully sent (true) or failed (false).

message_id.description: A unique integer identifier assigned to the sent message, used for retrieval or deletion operations.

receiver_id.description: The user ID of the message recipient, confirming who received the message.

sent_at.description: The timestamp when the message was sent, formatted as YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.

message.description: A human-readable status message describing the outcome of the send operation.

tools.name: view_inbox
tools.description: Use this tool to view all messages received by the currently logged-in user. Supports pagination via limit and offset parameters. Requires authentication via login. Returns an array of received message objects.

limit.description: Maximum number of messages to return in a single call, used to control pagination and response size. Defaults to 50 if not specified.

offset.description: Number of messages to skip from the beginning of the result set, used for pagination. Defaults to 0 if not specified.

message_id.description: Unique identifier of the received message, used for retrieval or deletion operations.

sender_id.description: The user ID of the message sender.

sender_username.description: The username of the message sender, if available.

message.description: The content of the received message.

received_at.description: The timestamp when the message was received, formatted as YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.

tools.name: view_sent_messages
tools.description: Use this tool to view all messages sent by the currently logged-in user. Optionally filter by receiver and supports pagination. Requires authentication via login. Returns an array of sent message objects.

receiver_id.description: Optional filter to restrict results to messages sent to a specific user ID. If not provided, returns messages to all recipients.

limit.description: Maximum number of messages to return in a single call, used to control pagination and response size. Defaults to 50 if not specified.

offset.description: Number of messages to skip from the beginning of the result set, used for pagination. Defaults to 0 if not specified.

message_id.description: Unique identifier of the sent message, used for retrieval or deletion operations.

receiver_id.description: The user ID of the message recipient.

receiver_username.description: The username of the message recipient, if available.

message.description: The content of the sent message.

sent_at.description: The timestamp when the message was sent, formatted as YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.

tools.name: get_message
tools.description: Use this tool to retrieve detailed information about a specific message by its unique ID. Returns complete message details including sender, receiver, content, timestamp, and read status for received messages.

message_id.description: The unique identifier of the message to retrieve. This ID is obtained from previous message operations like send_message, view_inbox, or view_sent_messages.
The unique identifier of the retrieved message.

sender_id.description: The user ID of the message sender.

receiver_id.description: The user ID of the message recipient.

sent_at.description: The timestamp when the message was sent, formatted as YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.

is_read.description: Indicates whether the message has been read by the recipient. This field is only present for received messages.

tools.name: delete_message
tools.description: Use this tool to delete a message that the user has sent or received. Requires authentication via login. Returns deletion status and the message ID.

message_id.description: The unique identifier of the message to delete. This ID is obtained from previous message operations and must reference a message the user has permission to delete.

success.description: Indicates whether the message was successfully deleted (true) or failed (false).

message_id.description: The unique identifier of the message that was deleted.

message.description: A human-readable status message describing the outcome of the deletion operation.

tools.name: search_messages
tools.description: Use this tool to search for messages containing a specific keyword in the current user's message history. Searches both sent and received messages with optional sender/receiver filters. Requires authentication via login. Returns an array of matching message objects.

keyword.description: The search term to find within message content. The tool will return messages containing this keyword in either sent or received messages.

sender_id.description: Optional filter to restrict search results to messages sent by a specific user ID. If not provided, searches across all senders.

receiver_id.description: Optional filter to restrict search results to messages sent to a specific user ID. If not provided, searches across all recipients.

limit.description: Maximum number of search results to return. Used to control response size and defaults to 20 if not specified.

message_id.description: Unique identifier of the matching message, used for retrieval or deletion operations.

sender_id.description: The user ID of the message sender.

receiver_id.description: The user ID of the message recipient.

message.description: The content of the message that matched the search keyword.

sent_at.description: The timestamp when the message was sent, formatted as YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.

tools.name: get_message_stats
tools.description: Use this tool to retrieve messaging statistics for the currently logged-in user. Optionally specify a time period to filter results. Requires authentication via login. Returns message counts and contact interaction metrics.

time_period.description: The time period for which to calculate statistics. Valid values are 'today', 'week', 'month', 'year', or 'all'. Defaults to 'all' if not specified.

total_sent.description: The total number of messages sent by the current user during the specified time period.

total_received.description: The total number of messages received by the current user during the specified time period.

total_contacts.description: The number of unique contacts the user has interacted with during the specified time period.

messages_by_contact.description: A dictionary mapping user IDs to message counts, showing the distribution of messages per contact. This field is optional and may not be present in all responses.
